title: Prevent XXE Attacks on XML Parsers
description: This rule is designed to prevent XML parsers from being vulnerable to XML External Entity (XXE) attacks. These vulnerabilities can lead to sensitive data exposure if malicious XML payloads are crafted to reference external entities that contain sensitive information such as system files, database credentials, or configuration files. Therefore, it's crucial to ensure that these vulnerabilities are remedied to maintain the confidentiality of critical information.
recommendation: Disable the use of external entities in XML parsing or take adequate precautions to prevent XXE vulnerabilities. Regularly assess and update your security protocols to guard against such attacks.
impact: Preventing XXE attacks is crucial for maintaining data confidentiality and security. If left unchecked, these vulnerabilities can lead to unauthorized access to sensitive data, posing a serious threat to your system's security.

checks.message: Disable the use of external entities in XML parsing or take adequate precautions to prevent XXE vulnerabilities. Regularly assess and update your security protocols to guard against such attacks.
